skills.name: msdmd
skills.description: Module Self-Declared Metadata in Markdown — the foundational convention where each source module declares its own structured metadata in a fenced comment block. Foundation for doc-build, cap-build, deps-build, owner-build, test-build, meta-module-build, risk-boundary-build, ratios, manifest, llms-build, and future msdmd applications.

skills.name: doc-build
skills.description: Self-declaring documentation coverage built on msdmd. Modules declare DOCS blocks; a runner verifies documentation paths and anchors, reports stale or missing docs, and surfaces visible coverage gaps.

skills.name: cap-build
skills.description: Self-declaring capability inventory built on msdmd. Modules declare CAPABILITIES blocks; a runner builds a capability map, verifies exposed surfaces, reports duplicates, and surfaces visible gaps.

skills.name: deps-build
skills.description: Self-declaring dependency topology built on msdmd. Modules declare DEPENDENCIES blocks; a runner builds import/call/capability graphs, detects unresolved edges and cycles, and surfaces visible gaps.

skills.name: owner-build
skills.description: Self-declaring module stewardship built on msdmd. Modules declare OWNERS blocks; a runner reports unowned modules, unresolved hmmm owners, and review coverage gaps.

skills.name: test-build
skills.description: Self-declaring contract evidence built on msdmd. Source modules declare behavior obligations in CONTRACTS blocks; test modules declare executable witnesses in CHECKS blocks; audit reconciles the witness list against the obligation list.

skills.name: meta-module-build
skills.description: Metadata-first module build skill built on msdmd. Use when turning a capability idea into a bounded module manifest, file plan, public/internal surface, permission boundary, tests, docs, rollout, and rollback notes before implementation.

skills.name: risk-boundary-build
skills.description: Self-declaring runtime risk and permission boundaries built on msdmd. Modules declare BOUNDARIES blocks for auth, storage, network, user-data, admin, and operational effects.

skills.name: ratios
skills.description: Self-declaring executable/source module composition ratios built on msdmd. Records lines of code to lines commented, imports to exports, and calls to definitions in a single `ratios:` comment line on covered source files' first and last line (not a fenced block; JSON/Markdown are out of scope); a runner recomputes values, fails on drift or misplacement, and reports visible source coverage gaps.

skills.name: canon
skills.description: Canonical-source and doctrine maintenance for The Interdependency skill library. Load when deciding whether a claim, pattern, workflow, or repo-local practice should become canon, or when preserving unresolved doctrine as hmmm.

skills.name: manifest
skills.description: Living-spec generator. Derives observable repo facts (name/version, Python floor, runtime deps, license, top-level layout) from pyproject.toml + the file tree and splices them into a machine-owned marked block in CLAUDE.md, with a CI --check drift gate. Use to keep the factual half of a doc generated rather than hand-typed.

skills.name: llms-build
skills.description: Self-declaring LLM instructions file built on msdmd. Modules or central files declare LLMS blocks; the python -m llms.build runner aggregates them into a standardized root llms.txt and surfaces drift/gaps.

skills.name: char-compress
skills.description: Character-based context compression for agent handoff and skill writing, derived from Unit Circle Number System compression mathematics. Load when compressing a long thread, document, repo audit, canon handoff, or agent working-memory state; when writing flesh-dense SKILL.md files; or when checking whether compression deleted negation, order, quantifier, operator, named object, value, decision, or unresolved hmmm.

skills.name: visitor-intro
skills.description: Onboarding tour for visitors arriving at any The-Interdependency repo. Load when an unfamiliar user asks 'what is this?', 'where do I start?', or otherwise signals they are new to the org.

skills.name: agent-instantiation
skills.description: Methodology for instantiating, forking, running, merging, and retiring agents in the a0 platform and its near-identical mirror a0ucns. Load when adding or changing a sub-agent spawn path, a PCNA instance fork/merge, an agent definition or naming scheme, spawn caps or approval gating, an agent run/log table, a heartbeat agent task, or an agent-state checkpoint. a0-betatest (a0p) has diverged to a per-user CRUD + native-ZFAE model and is out of scope. Repo-specific runtime doctrine; canonical source is a0. Independent of msdmd.

skills.name: a0p-instancing
skills.description: Methodology for instancing agents in a0-betatest (the a0p research instrument), whose model diverges from canonical a0. Load when adding or changing an AgentInstance / CharacterSheet CRUD path, a per-instance native ZFAE weight bank or its teacher-distillation training, a ZFAE inference mode, a sentinel evaluation or pending-override gate, a per-agent safetensors checkpoint, or volatile sub-context memory under a0-betatest backend/. a0p has no sub_agent_spawn, spawn executor, or InstanceMerge; for canonical a0/a0ucns use agent-instantiation instead. Repo-specific runtime doctrine; canonical source is a0-betatest. Independent of msdmd.

skills.name: plain-lens
skills.description: Plain-language, multi-lens companion views of dense canonical text — easier on-ramps that do not replace or talk down to the source. Load when making dense documents approachable, building a domain/audience/role lens selector, designing progressive-disclosure reading UX, keeping a static page as fallback for a dynamic one, or producing an EDCM-style two-speaker tension reading between a body text and its footnotes.

skills.name: meta
skills.description: Meta Energy Theory Axioms. Load when extracting, preserving, extending, or applying Energy Theory axioms and cross-domain resonances without flattening Energy Theory into EDCMBONE or FLAR implementation detail.

skills.name: gonal-morphology
skills.description: Canonical three-core gonal morphology: text as UCNS objects across a char->root/bone/word->phrase/clause ladder under one carrier-LCM operator (⊠ = UCNS multiplyFuel). omega=bones (closed-class+affixes), phi=roots (open-class stems), psi=words=phi⊠omega; adjectives/adverbs are flesh, not bones. Reuse the UCNS operator, do not reimplement. Load for gonal morphology, inscription, or bone/flesh classification. Recomposition RUNS; decomposition is domain-confirmed (UCNS AlignedComplete) but proof-pending — not machine-verified. Independent of msdmd.

skills.name: the-interdependency
skills.description: Protocol and workflow for all tasks involving The Interdependency organization, its repositories, The Interdependent Way projects, EDCMBONE transcript analysis, code building, research, GitHub maintenance and updates.

skills.name: interdependent-work-graph
skills.description: Cross-repository coordination for The Interdependency. Load when a task spans, consumes, compares, publishes to, or can change the contract between two or more repositories; when an agent is about to choose one repo as its workspace for a stack-level problem; when exact producer, evidence-source, skill, semantic, mathematical, or measurement identities must travel together; or when creating a shared stack manifest, multi-repo handoff, coordinated release, or cross-repo validation plan.

skills.name: loop-eng
skills.description: Loop engineering for designing closed feedback cycles (Discover→Plan→Execute→Verify→Iterate), single-agent and fleet loops with subagent maker/checker separation, and automated verify-iterate workflows.

skills.name: skill-build
skills.description: Skill authoring and skill compliance workflow for The Interdependency skill-lib. Load this when creating a new SKILL.md, revising an existing skill, bringing repo skills into a shared compliance shape, designing a skill-specific test suite, deciding whether a skill is metadata-block or procedural, or asking the question set required before a skill can be accepted.

skills.name: typed-meta-frontend
skills.description: TypeScript frontend generation from backend-owned module metadata and living specs. Load this when building, reviewing, or refactoring a self-building UI that reads backend metadata, exposes every editable field per module, renders each module's living spec, generates TypeScript types/forms/routes from metadata, or keeps admin/editor frontends synchronized with msdmd-style source declarations.
